items.title: Anker’s originally $100 MagSafe 3-in-1 Qi2 15W desktop charging station is just $35 today (65% off)
items.summary:
As part of its wider sale on charging accessories, Woot is now offering Anker’s 3-in-1 15W Qi2 MagGo wireless charging pad in black for $34.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee applies otherwise). This is originally a $100 accessory designed to charge your iPhone, AirPods, and Apple Watch all at once. While Amazon currently has a “Used: Like New” listing at $47.99 shipped, Woot’s deal gets you a brand-new unit for considerably less. This is the lowest price we have tracked for this charger, landing it $2 below its previous all-time low. Head below for more details.

items.summary:
BougeRV Movable Area Light

Buy for $24 (Reg. $40)

What we love

If you’ve got outdoor summer adventures awaiting you, this BougeRV portable area light would make a handy addition to your kit, though it’s also a convenient device to have year-round, too. It possesses an internal 3,200mAh battery that can handle lighting spaces, tents, more for up to six nights on each charge, complete with five adjustable brightness levels, four lighting modes, and an emergency S.O.S. signal. What’s more, it has the double function to power personal devices as a power bank.

items.title: Segway’s GT3 electric superscooter is a more affordable thrill-ride packed with features at its $1,500 low
items.summary:
There are only a handful of days left to jump on Segway’s Summer EV Sale deals, and rather than spending over $2,000 on the latest supercharged e-scooter, you can instead go with the GT3 Electric SuperScooter for $1,499.99 shipped, matching the price we’re seeing over at Amazon. Despite its $1,900 MSRP, you can usually find this model starting at $1,700, with 2026 having given us discounts down to $1,637 before this rate became the more frequent price point we’ve seen during sales. For the rest of May, you can jump on it with a $200 markdown off the going rate (and $400 off the MSRP), giving you a more thrill-inducing ride for the all-time lowest price we have tracked, which lands $1,000 under the newest GT3 Pro Electric SuperScooter currently.

items.summary:

This particular model hit the scene towards the end of last year, carrying a $600 price tag, and today’s offer shaves a hefty $280 off that going rate. It’s now undercutting its previous best price of $400 from earlier this year by $80, marking the lowest price we’ve tracked yet for this monitor. The same monitor, in case you’re wondering, is currently going for $450 at Amazon. Head below for more details.

This particular model from HP’s OmniBook 3 lineup normally carries a $950 price tag, but today’s offer knocks $370 off to make it easier on your wallet. We’ve previously seen the AMD-powered variant of this laptop go for less, but this is the lowest we have seen this model fetch to date. It doesn’t get much less than this for a touchscreen 2K laptop, so be sure to grab this while the offer is live.

items.title: Silkland’s compact 240W USB/Thunderbolt 4 cable hits $13.50
items.summary:
Silkland 240W 40Gb/s USB/Thunderbolt 4 Cable

Buy for $13.50 (Reg. $16)

What we love

Long cables can be super handy, but in my opinion the same can be said for short cords that get straight to the point without a bunch of unwanted slack just cluttering things up. It spans just one foot in length, which could be perfect for a desk setup or a quick and easy cable that won’t take up much space in your travel bag.

items.summary:
Acer 2-in-1 Type-C SD Card Reader

Buy for $9.50 (Reg. $13)

What we love

I have four HomeKit security cameras that have motion clips in the cloud, but they also record to a microSD card around the clock so I can take a deeper dive if I ever need to. There have been times when I needed to, and that’s precisely why I am happy I have a similar card reader in my tech bin.
